ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Fɛɛ Yesu ǝtǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Fǝǝ, tɔnɔ lǝ eyifo nnya nko te mɔmɔ. Itsyise nko fɛ yuutǝ buyifo lesa nǝ ni Onanto eto lelabi nɛ.” Nyaso Yohanes átɔnɔ útǝ wǝ.
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, "Go, the harvest is plentiful but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field." John also was baptizing.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ leeyifo Onanto eto lelabii, wǝ ni mɔ ɔyimisani nye mɔ ɔyimisio, nye mɔ ambe nɛ.”
translated: God created the world and everything in it, he created the male and the female, and the female was given to the male.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Itsyise lǝ nnya kamaa, bensiyifo betidi inuǝ. Bomu bafiani utidi ɔni. Nyaso yanle bǝ utidi sense lesa nǝ Onanto dífi ɔ́kɔ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ.”
translated: The elders say that when a person is about to die, they will speak the truth. The person will reveal all the bad things they did. The truth will come out and everyone will know, and God will judge all the bad things they did.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Yanklɛ fɛ ufiebi ko dítsyiko bɔsɔ bembembe nya ɔ́sɔ ntu ókpo lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo, nya ibu sɛkɛ lǝ ntu lǝ buyi ɔklɔ ǝmǝ lǝ onya udu fǝ lǝ ntu kasɔ.
translated: The farmer went to the forest to cut sticks to build a fence around his farm, and also to get sticks to build a fence around his house and to make a door for his house.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Utsyuǝ saa ke loofo mii, mɔ ofo. Nya utsyuǝ saa ke loofo mɛɛ, ofo nwǝ létsyese mɛ tsya.
translated: The stranger is coming to our house, let us go. When the stranger comes to our house, we will give him water to drink.

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Fɛɛ mɔmɔɔ, nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ bela mi balo lǝ beto ola lǝ mba leekisi mi eto disi.
translated: The woman's mother, she gave me a basket full of yams to take to her daughter who was at the farm.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝ dimǝ laanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ.
translated: The child is crying because he fell and hurt his leg. The woman is going to the forest to get medicine to heal the wound.

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Be bísu kabekɔ fɛɛ? Utidi wǝ nkpe bikǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ bia laanɔ bobe lee? Betidi ba laakpe bikǝ biamfo eto oklee, Lekayo baakɛ.
translated: What is the name of this town? How did the people of old used to call this place? The people of old used to call this place Lekayo.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Nyaso ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Mi tsya su biyifo keyifo lǝ mɔ eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ lǝ mfo mi kayo ka dińyǝ itǝ.’
translated: The woman said to me, 'I will not allow the bad spirits of the forest to kill my child and take him away from me.'

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Lefe kpǝ lǝ wǝ asatete ǝsuǝǝ, údi etiki ɔ́yɛnko lǝ akpa ǝsuǝ lǝ ufi ulǝkǝ asa nnya óla nkǝ loote mǝ kasɔ. Asa nnya óte mǝ ni nkǝ.
translated: The farmer went to the forest, he took a cutlass and a basket and went to look for a yam to eat, but he couldn't find one. He didn't find one to eat.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Oteasa, nte kɔfi ko nso bofe lǝ afi nyamǝ kenke nti?”
translated: “Oteasa, why do you always bring your dirty feet to my compound?”

ref: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
text: Ókpa Petro ku Sebedeo eto bebi inuǝ bamǝ ɔ́yɛnko. Údufǝ lǝ kawɛndo ǝsuǝ, nya útsyiko butinkǝ kekleke.
translated: Mr. Petro and Sebedeo went to the forest to hunt monkeys. They took their cutlasses and dogs, and also their guns to shoot.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Lefe nǝ besio bamfó dísinkli bantɔɔ, bamamanyǝ ba lése kudi komǝ ésu umǝ nya bátɛyi besumunyǝ ninǝ bamǝ asa nnya kenke díbǝ mfó.
translated: They say that the ancestors of old were very strong, our grandfathers were able to cultivate the land with their hands and make their own food and build their own houses and make their own palm oil.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Mɔ Anto éfi asa nnya kenke útǝ mɛ. Kuutsyuǝ saa enyi Ubi ǝmǝ enso Anto. Nya kuutsyuǝ saa enyi Anto ǝmǝ enso Ubi ǝmǝ ku mba Ubi ǝmǝ laala nkǝ wǝǝlǝkǝ wǝ oté.
translated: Anto's mother said she would give kenke to the child. Ubi's father went to Anto's place. Anto's father went to Ubi's place and said Ubi's child should come and collect her slippers under the tree.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ lekpá bamba nkǝ, “Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ale utsyuǝ wǝ díse eyibibi sɛɛ lǝ wǝ ɔkuɛ.
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, "Whoever wants to be first among you will be a slave to everyone."

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Wǝ asatete ményifo fe kase afi eto bateasa laate. Bomu óte asa fe nwǝ nkpe ǝsuǝale lǝ asa nnya óte ǝsuǝ.
translated: They are going to the forest to cut sticks to build a house. The men will cut the sticks and the women will carry them on their heads.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Lǝ bati mi kama lǝ umǝ ɔnii, bitsyetsyi bisu umǝ bamba. Nya nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ, biembuyifo mi keyifo belosǝ lǝ Israel eto kǝmǝ ka kenke fɛ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi luubǝ.
translated: They said I am like the son of a king, that is why they are doing this to me. But I know I am a poor person, they are trying to make me a scapegoat like the people of Israel did to that man, Jesus Christ.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Kɔɔ bɛnɔ tsya bátɛyi betidi bǝnkǝ, ‘Mankpadi asa esi etudi kɛnyɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ. Bomu yifo lǝ ntamu bo faka fǝtǝ Bonamute ǝsuǝ.’
translated: The elder said to the children, 'The hunter's wife is not allowed to eat the meat of the Bonamute animal. If she does, her husband will not catch any more Bonamute.'

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Se ɔntɔɔ, usionko utsyuǝ wǝ kɔtɔ díkakpo lǝ lefosi alɛ ǝnuǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ ǝsuǝ tsya ǝtǝkǝko Yesu kama nya útidi wǝ awu eto kotosi.
translated: When he came, he did not find him, but he found the disciples sleeping and said to them, "Why are you sleeping, get up, let us go, the betrayer of Jesus is near, the hour has come, let us go to the cross."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Itsyise utidi wǝ nkpe asaa, wǝ ke bonsi boofi ditsyuǝ bakpe bawunsǝ nɛ. Nya utidi wǝ kulesa lentee, sɛkɛɛ nte wǝ tsyaa, bɔɔlɛ ni bafo wǝ lǝ ani.
translated: The farmer went to the forest, he cut down trees to make a farm but the trees were too many for him. The farmer went to the village elder, he explained to him, the elder said he should call his friends to help him.
